name: Papua New Guinea
avoid: November–April is wetter, muddier, and more disruption-prone for highlands roads, village access, and volcano viewpoints.

Papua New Guinea needs a stronger safety buffer than most Pacific itineraries: use hotel-arranged airport transfers, avoid walking after dark, skip casual PMV use in Port Moresby, and keep valuables out of sight.
Domestic flights on Air Niugini and PNG Air are often the only practical links between provinces, so build in at least one same-day buffer before any international departure.
stopoverNote: Port Moresby is the transport anchor for almost every Papua New Guinea trip, so the smoothest plan is one capital block, one PMV or private-road highlands leg between Mount Hagen and Goroka, and then one final domestic flight out to East New Britain.

cities.name: Port Moresby
cities.notes: The capital is the safest place to sort SIMs, cash, domestic flights, and guided day trips to Varirata National Park, the National Museum, and Kokoda pre-trek logistics.

cities.name: Mount Hagen
cities.notes: Western Highlands base for the Mount Hagen Cultural Show, produce markets, Waghi Valley landscapes, and the short detour to the UNESCO-listed Kuk Early Agricultural Site.

cities.name: Goroka
cities.notes: Goroka mixes Eastern Highlands museums, coffee country, and the best access to Asaro Mudmen villages and Daulo Pass viewpoints.

cities.name: Rabaul/Kokopo
cities.notes: East New Britain's twin-base stop combines Kokopo seaside hotels with Rabaul's caldera history, Tavurvur volcano outings, Baining performances, and onward diving links.

itinerary.sim: Digicel PNG is the most reliable tourist SIM for Port Moresby, the Highlands, and East New Britain, with bmobile-Vodafone as the usual backup; buy and top up in Port Moresby before you fly onward.

Port Moresby.name: Port Moresby
Port Moresby.note: Keep the capital tightly structured around museum time, a guided Varirata run, and flight logistics rather than trying to sightsee independently across the city.

days.theme: Museum Collections, Nature Park and an Easy Capital Arrival

activities.name: Papua New Guinea National Museum and Art Gallery
activities.tip: Adult entry is commonly around PGK15–20, and it is the easiest first stop for Sepik carvings, masks, and wartime history.

activities.name: Port Moresby Nature Park
activities.tip: Budget about PGK40 for adult entry; go earlier in the day for tree kangaroos, cassowaries, and shaded bird enclosures.

activities.name: Ela Beach and APEC Haus seafront walk

days.theme: Varirata Rainforest, War Memory and Trek Logistics

activities.name: Varirata National Park
activities.tip: The official expatriate adult park fee is PGK10, with vehicle charges usually around PGK50–100 depending on size, so it works best as a guided shared trip.

activities.name: Kokoda Track permit processing with a licensed operator
activities.tip: The Kokoda Track Authority's international trek permit is PGK350 per trekker, and licensed operators bundle it into the final trek package.

Mount Hagen.name: Mount Hagen
Mount Hagen.note: Mount Hagen works best as a short highlands culture stop, with the August show as the main draw and Kuk or market time as the fallback outside festival dates.

days.theme: Highlands Culture, Market Life and Kuk Side Trip

activities.name: Mount Hagen Cultural Show grounds pass
activities.tip: For the August show, independent tourist day passes are commonly budgeted around PGK150–300, and accommodation sells out long before the event.

activities.name: Mount Hagen Market walk
activities.tip: A hotel-arranged local escort is worth the small cost because the produce market is most comfortable earlier in the morning.

activities.name: Kuk Early Agricultural Site guided visit
activities.tip: Community guide and transport costs vary, but many day visits land around PGK50–100 per person before private transfer charges.

Goroka.name: Goroka
Goroka.note: Goroka is the most rewarding Eastern Highlands base if you pair one town-and-museum day with one dedicated Asaro or Daulo excursion.

days.theme: Museum History, Coffee Country and a Soft Highlands Afternoon

activities.name: J.K. McCarthy Museum
activities.tip: Entry is usually modest and the aviation, patrol-post, and highlands collections make the best context stop before village outings.

activities.name: Raun Raun Theatre and Cultural Centre
activities.tip: Expect a small community donation or arranged guide fee if a rehearsal, music session, or craft stop is available.

activities.name: Goroka market and coffee tasting stop

days.theme: Asaro Mudmen and Daulo Pass Viewpoints

activities.name: Asaro Mudmen village performance
activities.tip: Most day tours or direct village arrangements work out near PGK250–400 per visitor depending on group size, transport, and whether dances are private.

activities.name: Daulo Pass lookout and ridge stop
activities.tip: A small local guide or land-access payment is common if you stop for photography and short ridge walks.

Rabaul/Kokopo.name: Rabaul/Kokopo
Rabaul/Kokopo.note: Base yourself in Kokopo for easier hotels, then day-trip into Rabaul for volcanoes, war history, and the Baining cultural night.

days.theme: Caldera Orientation, War Relics and the Kokopo Waterfront

activities.name: Rabaul Volcano Observatory lookout
activities.tip: Most visits only need a small donation or local guiding fee, and it is the best quick overview of the caldera and Tavurvur.

days.theme: Tavurvur Volcano, Hot Springs and Old Rabaul

activities.name: Mount Tavurvur volcano excursion
activities.tip: Recent guided half-day or cruise-market rates have commonly landed around AUD85–100 per adult, usually including transport and a local guide.

activities.name: Old Rabaul town and market drive

days.theme: Baining Fire Dance or a Kimbe Bay Dive Extension

activities.name: Baining Fire Dance evening tour
activities.tip: Recent organized Gaulim-village tours have been quoted around AUD135 per adult including transport, security, water, and guiding.

activities.name: Walindi Plantation Resort two-tank Kimbe Bay dive day
activities.tip: Recent published Kimbe Bay dive pricing has typically sat around PGK470–600 before equipment rental, flights, or overnight stays in West New Britain.

connections.method: Hotel-arranged transfer or licensed airport taxi to Waigani, Ela Beach, or airport-side hotels; this is preferable to using casual transport after arrival.
Air Niugini or PNG Air domestic flight from Jacksons to Kagamuga; flying is effectively essential because road connections from the capital are not practical.
Highlands Highway PMV or hotel-arranged road transfer; PMVs are the classic local option, but most visitors prefer a private day transfer for comfort and security.
Air Niugini or PNG Air domestic flight, usually via Port Moresby, into Tokua Airport for Kokopo and Rabaul.
Air Niugini or PNG Air flight from Tokua back to Port Moresby for onward international departures.

extras: Keep most cash in PNG kina for markets, museum tickets, village fees, and PMVs; card acceptance is best only at the larger hotels in Port Moresby, Goroka, and Kokopo.
The Kokoda Track Authority's current international trek permit is PGK350 (about ₹7,350) per trekker, and treks must be arranged through a licensed operator rather than independently.

Port Moresby needs the strongest safety discipline in the itinerary: move by hotel transfer or trusted taxi, avoid isolated walks, and treat PMVs in the capital as a local-only option unless you are with a trusted fixer.
Air Niugini and PNG Air schedules can shift with weather or equipment changes, so keep all luggage tagged carefully and avoid booking your final domestic hop on the same day as a long-haul departure.
